title: Ownership, Borrowing, Aliasing, and API Contracts · Object-Lifetime Lens: Alias Mutation Policy
summary: A media index passes buffers through parsers, caches, and callbacks where no component knows who must release or may mutate them. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem alias mutation policy increment in ownership-annotated buffer API. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prevent surprising mutation through aliases with interface design, copying, or explicit synchronization.
Bound every borrow by owner lifetime, mutation rules, callback duration, and asynchronous retention.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Alias Mutation Policy · Theory Alias Mutation Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 9832. Produce bounded C evidence to track owner, borrow, mutation, and release state; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prevent surprising mutation through aliases with interface design, copying, or explicit synchronization.
